title: Refining the Modeled Objects
breadcrumbs: Design Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Refining the Modeled Objects After completing the access path analysis, you can choose to refine the modeled entities, fields, keys, and relationships. For example, you can: Refine the model to reflect the business more accurately Eliminate superfluous entities, attributes, and relationships If mistakes were made during the analysis phase of the application life cycle, there might be entities, relationships, or fields that are never used by users or by the system itself. Prototyping can reveal whether these fields are really needed now or for future requirements. De-normalize, for example by adding relationships. When there is no optimal access path, it is possible to add redundant data or introduce relationships to meet the access requirements. For more information, see Minimizing Access Paths .
